theme: Intent-to-action pipelines meet autonomous simulation loops — agents that plan, execute, test, and iterate in closed-loop training environments.

candidates.title: Hi Robot: Open-Ended Instruction Following with Hierarchical Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.summary: Hierarchical VLA architecture that decomposes open-ended natural language instructions into executable robot subtasks with online adaptation. Combines task planning with vision-language grounding for generalist robot control. — https://openreview.net/forum?id=lNVHg9npif

candidates.title: Causal World Modeling for Robot Control
candidates.summary: Fresh approach using autoregressive causal world models (LingBot-VA) that tightly integrate vision, language, and action for long-horizon rollouts. Demonstrates emergent long-range temporal memory and strong few-shot adaptation. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.21998

candidates.title: Interactive World Simulator for Robot Policy Training and Evaluation
candidates.summary: Proposes world simulators as zero-shot policies that enable continuous evaluation and iteration loops for robot learning. Bridges simulation and real-world deployment with interactive world models. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.08546

candidates.title: 3D-Grounded Vision-Language Framework for Robotic Task Planning: Automated Prompt Synthesis and Supervised Reasoning
candidates.summary: Achieves 67% improvement in 3D spatial grounding accuracy through automated prompt synthesis and expert supervision. Frozen VLMs with structured 3D reasoning for robot manipulation. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.08903

candidates.title: Agentic LLM-based robotic systems for real-world applications: a review on their agenticness and ethics
candidates.summary: Comprehensive 2025 review of agentic frameworks in robotics, including industry-specific vertical AI agent deployment patterns. Critical for understanding the middleware/orchestration layer. — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12402697/

candidates.title: Simplify Generalist Robot Policy Evaluation in Simulation with NVIDIA Isaac Lab-Arena
candidates.summary: End-to-end workflow for evaluating Isaac GR00T N models in Isaac Lab-Arena. Shows practical sim-in-the-loop eval for generalist policies with continuous performance optimization. — https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/simplify-generalist-robot-policy-evaluation-in-simulation-with-nvidia-isaac-lab-arena

candidates.title: Digital twins to embodied artificial intelligence: review and perspective
candidates.summary: Positions digital twins as the training and evaluation substrate for embodied AI. Emphasizes data generation for safe physical-world interaction. — https://www.oaepublish.com/articles/ir.2025.11

candidates.title: RoboGen: Towards Unleashing Infinite Data for Automated Robot Learning via Generative Simulation
candidates.summary: Self-guided propose-generate-learn cycle where the agent proposes tasks, generates simulation scenes, labels with NL, and trains supervision. Generative sim as autonomous learning engine. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.01455

candidates.title: Physically Grounded Vision-Language Models for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.summary: Fine-tunes VLMs on physically grounded concepts (mass, friction, deformability) and integrates with LLM-based planners. Improved performance on tasks requiring physical reasoning. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.02561

candidates.title: A Review of Nine Physics Engines for Reinforcement Learning Research
candidates.summary: Comparative analysis of MuJoCo, PhysX/Isaac Gym, PyBullet, and others for RL-driven robot training. Practical guide for choosing sim platforms based on fidelity, speed, and customization. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.08590

theme: Causal world models meet spatial reasoning — infrastructure for intent-to-action pipelines and simulation-driven robot learning.

candidates.title: Interactive World Simulator for Robot Policy Training and Evaluation
candidates.summary: World model as interactive simulator for zero-shot policy learning and continuous evaluation in robotics — https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.08546

candidates.title: RoboSpatial: Teaching Spatial Understanding to 2D and 3D Vision-Language Models
candidates.summary: Framework teaching VLMs precise spatial reasoning for robotics (spatial affordance prediction, manipulation planning) — https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.16537

candidates.title: Digital Twins Transition to Intelligent, AI-Driven Systems in 2026
candidates.summary: Edge AI + digital twins for millisecond-level autonomy in robotics, reducing cloud reliance for real-time adaptive control — https://www.rtinsights.com/digital-twins-in-2026-from-digital-replicas-to-intelligent-ai-driven-systems/

candidates.title: Digital Twins to Embodied AI: Review and Perspective
candidates.summary: Digital twins as safe training/evaluation environment for embodied AI, realistic data generation for complex environments — https://www.oaepublish.com/articles/ir.2025.11

candidates.title: Agentic LLM-based Robotic Systems: Review on Agenticness and Ethics
candidates.summary: Comprehensive review of agentic systems in robotics, deployment of vertical AI agents for industry applications — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12402697/

candidates.title: A-RAG: Scaling Agentic RAG via Hierarchical Retrieval Interfaces
candidates.summary: Agentic RAG framework with hierarchical retrieval for complex tasks (predefined workflows, multi-iteration) — https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.03442

candidates.title: Simplify Generalist Robot Policy Evaluation in Simulation with NVIDIA Isaac Lab-Arena
candidates.summary: End-to-end workflow for evaluating generalist policies (Isaac GR00T N) in simulation, manipulation skill testing — https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/simplify-generalist-robot-policy-evaluation-in-simulation-with-nvidia-isaac-lab-arena/

candidates.title: Reinforcement Learning in Robotic Systems: Review on Sim-to-Real Transfer
candidates.summary: Sim-to-real RL framework for model optimization and iterative policy transfer in robotics — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0921889025004245

candidates.title: Hi Robot: Open-Ended Instruction Following with Hierarchical VLMs
candidates.summary: Hierarchical vision-language-action model for open-ended instruction following (reasoning + execution for intricate NL instructions) — https://openreview.net/forum?id=lNVHg9npif

theme: Causal world modeling, digital twins, and agentic robot middleware converge as foundations for intent-driven robot action.

candidates.title: Causal World Modeling for Robot Control
candidates.summary: Vision-language action diffusion models (LingBot-VA) integrate autoregressive causal world models for long-horizon rollouts with closed-loop error correction. Emergent long-range temporal memory and few-shot adaptation—a fresh foundation for robot learning beyond VLM pre-training alone. — https://ar

candidates.title: Digital twins to embodied artificial intelligence: review and perspective
candidates.summary: Comprehensive review bridging digital twins to embodied AI, focusing on data generation for safe robot training and evaluation. Addresses the real-world interaction problem with digital twin simulations at scale. — https://www.oaepublish.com/articles/ir.2025.11

candidates.title: 3D-Grounded Vision-Language Framework for Robotic Task Planning: Automated Prompt Synthesis and Supervised Reasoning
candidates.summary: Frozen VLMs achieve high-precision 3D grounding through automated prompt synthesis and expert supervision, improving spatial reasoning for robot control by 67%. Bridges vision-language understanding and 3D spatial action planning. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.08903

candidates.title: GRADE: Generating Realistic and Dynamic Environments for robotics research with Isaac Sim
candidates.summary: Flexible, controllable framework for photorealistic robotic environment generation in NVIDIA Isaac Sim. Addresses integration of diverse assets (UE, Blender, AutoCAD) into USD format for simulation pipelines—enables large-scale synthetic data generation. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.04466

candidates.title: A Review of Natural-Language-Instructed Robot Execution Systems
candidates.summary: Survey covering NL instruction → robot execution pipelines, task collaboration frameworks, and integration patterns for high-level task planning with embodied execution. Solid foundation for intent-to-action systems. — https://www.mdpi.com/2673-2688/5/3/48

candidates.title: Agentic LLM-based robotic systems for real-world applications: a review on their agenticness and ethics
candidates.summary: Comprehensive review on agentic LLM robotics systems, covering deployment strategies, vertical AI agents for industry-specific applications, and ethical considerations for embodied agency. — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12402697/

candidates.title: RoboGen: Towards Unleashing Infinite Data for Automated Robot Learning via Generative Simulation
candidates.summary: Generative robotic agent that self-proposes skills, generates scene configurations, labels tasks with NL, and designs training supervisions—all autonomously in simulation. Scalable data generation for diverse skill learning. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.01455

candidates.title: Reinforcement learning in robotic systems: A review on sim-to-real transfer
candidates.summary: Presents sim-to-real RL framework for model optimization and iterative policy transfer. Addresses the reality gap with systematic methods for domain adaptation and transfer validation. — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0921889025004245

candidates.title: Unreal Robotics Lab: A High-Fidelity Robotics Simulator with Advanced Physics and Rendering
candidates.summary: Unreal Engine-based simulator combining high-precision physics with photorealistic rendering for vision-based navigation. Fills gap between MuJoCo-level physics and Unreal-level visuals—enables synthetic rare-event scenario generation. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.14135

candidates.title: Digital Twin based Automatic Reconfiguration of Robotic Systems in Smart Environments
candidates.summary: Uses SLAM, Unity3D, and Gazebo for digital twin-driven automatic robot reconfiguration in smart environments. Practical integration of spatial mapping and path planning with simulation-based validation. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.00094

theme: Interactive world simulators, game-engine robotics ecosystems, and spatial reasoning benchmarks for embodied AI

candidates.title: Interactive World Simulator for Robot Policy Training and Evaluation
candidates.summary: Fast, stable world models for robot learning with 10-minute+ interactions at 15 FPS; policies trained on generated data match real-world performance — https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.08546

candidates.title: SpatialBench: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Spatial Cognition
candidates.summary: Hierarchical evaluation framework for spatial intelligence in MLLMs, from perception to causal planning — https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.21471

candidates.title: RoboSpatial: Teaching Spatial Understanding to 2D and 3D Vision-Language Models for Robotics
candidates.summary: 1M images, 5K 3D scans, 3M spatial relationships dataset improving VLM manipulation planning — https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.16537

candidates.title: Robotic World Model: A Neural Network Simulator for Robust Policy Optimization in Robotics
candidates.summary: Dual-autoregressive world model enabling policy learning without domain-specific biases, strong sim-to-real transfer — https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.10100

candidates.title: Digital twins to embodied artificial intelligence: review and perspective
candidates.summary: Comprehensive review of DT-Embodied AI integration for safe robot training and evaluation at scale — https://www.oaepublish.com/articles/ir.2025.11

candidates.title: GRADE: Generating Realistic and Dynamic Environments for robotics research with Isaac Sim
candidates.summary: Flexible, photorealistic, ROS-integrated Isaac Sim framework for robotics research environments — https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/02783649251346211

candidates.title: Unreal Robotics Lab: A High-Fidelity Robotics Simulator with Advanced Physics and Rendering
candidates.summary: Unreal Engine-based simulator bridging photorealism and high-precision physics for RL policy training — https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.14135

candidates.title: Hierarchical Open-Vocabulary 3D Scene Graphs for Language-Grounded Robot Navigation
candidates.summary: Open-vocabulary scene graphs with 75% size reduction vs. dense maps, tested on multi-story environments — https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.17846

candidates.title: Multi-Agent Coordination across Diverse Applications: A Survey
candidates.summary: Comprehensive survey of multi-agent systems including task decomposition for robot coordination and LLM-based orchestration — https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.14743

candidates.title: The Essential Role of Causality in Foundation World Models for Embodied AI
candidates.summary: Theoretical analysis of causal reasoning requirements for world models in embodied agents — https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.06665

theme: Intent-to-action pipelines meet spatial reasoning: from LLM task decomposition to 3D scene graphs for robotic manipulation.

candidates.title: MALLVi: Multi-Agent Framework for Integrated Generalized Robotics Manipulation
candidates.summary: Agentic multi-agent system where a "Thinker" LLM translates high-level subtasks into executable parameters for manipulation agents — direct implementation of intent decomposition for robot control. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.16898

candidates.title: Embodied-RAG: Non-parametric Memory for Indoor/Outdoor Navigation and Explanation
candidates.summary: Spatial RAG system capturing embodied memories at any semantic/spatial resolution, enabling retrieval-augmented navigation and language generation for embodied agents — bridges spatial memory with agentic reasoning. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.18313

candidates.title: RoboEXP: Action-Conditioned Scene Graph for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.summary: Interactive scene graph construction through exploration, providing prior object location knowledge + task feasibility reasoning (e.g., "no spoon → ask for help") — crucial for real-world indoor manipulation. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.15487

candidates.title: Open-Vocabulary Functional 3D Scene Graphs for Real-World Indoor Spaces
candidates.summary: Detects objects + interactive elements + functional relationships in indoor RGB-D sequences, enabling 3D question answering and robotic manipulation via functional scene graphs. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.19199

candidates.title: The Essential Role of Causality in Foundation World Models for Embodied AI
candidates.summary: Argues causal models are inherently high-accuracy predictors of action consequences, making them essential for world models — predictive models become causal when trained with appropriate algorithms/datasets. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.06665

candidates.title: Latent Action Robot Foundation World Models for Cross-Embodiment Adaptation
candidates.summary: Robot world model operating in a unified latent action space shared across diverse embodiments — improves adaptation to unseen robot platforms. — https://openreview.net/forum?id=vEZgPr1deb

candidates.title: N3D-VLM: Native 3D Grounding for Spatial Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
candidates.summary: VLM with intrinsic 3D object perception, enabling precise 3D grounding + interpretable spatial understanding — addresses VLMs' lack of depth/spatial relationship comprehension. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.16561

candidates.title: RoboSpatial: Teaching Spatial Understanding to 2D and 3D Vision-Language Models
candidates.summary: Training framework for spatial affordance prediction, spatial relationship prediction, and robotic manipulation using both 2D and 3D VLMs — addresses VLM spatial reasoning gaps. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.16537

candidates.title: Robot Simulation Software: A 2026 Perspective
candidates.summary: 2026 overview of Unity, Isaac Sim, MuJoCo, Gazebo — computation, photo-realism, physics accuracy, and application suitability for each platform. Timely comparative guide for autonomous sim loop practitioners. — https://www.blackcoffeerobotics.com/blog/which-robot-simulation-software-to-use

candidates.title: AffordGrasp: In-Context Affordance Reasoning for Open-Vocabulary Task-Oriented Grasping
candidates.summary: Framework identifies task-relevant objects, grounds part-level affordances via visual grounding, generates task-oriented grasp poses within affordance regions — functional, context-aware manipulation. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.00778

theme: Task decomposition, simulation scaffolding, and VLA reasoning dominate this week's crop — a continuation of the intent-to-action focus with fresh traction on multi-robot coordination and sim-to-real pipelines.

candidates.title: Scale-Plan: Scalable Language-Enabled Task Planning for Heterogeneous Multi-Robot Teams
candidates.summary: Outperforms LLM and hybrid LLM-PDDL baselines on complex multi-agent tasks; introduces MAT2-THOR benchmark for reliable multi-robot planning evaluation. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.08814

candidates.title: Large Language Models for Robotics: A survey
candidates.summary: Comprehensive survey covering LLM integration in embodied AI, including RoCo for multi-arm collaboration and task decomposition across 6 tasks. — https://arxiv.org/html/2311.07226v2

candidates.title: Vision-Language-Policy Model for Dynamic Robot Task Planning
candidates.summary: Outperforms VLA-based approaches in task decomposition and planning interpretability; combines VLMs with action policies for dynamic reasoning. — https://arxiv.org/html/2512.19178v1

candidates.title: DiT4DiT: Jointly Modeling Video Dynamics and Actions for Generalizable Robot Control
candidates.summary: Uses video generation models as foundation model backbone — trains on internet-scale data to learn motion priors, causal structure, and implicit physical dynamics. — https://arxiv.org/html/2603.10448v1

candidates.title: Scaling Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning for Robot VLAs with Generative 3D Worlds
candidates.summary: Leverages generative 3D world models to scale unlimited synthetic data; ablation shows increasing scene diversity directly improves zero-shot generalization. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18532

candidates.title: Language Movement Primitives: Grounding Language Models in Robot Motion
candidates.summary: Grounds VLMs into control-theoretic trajectories via dynamic movement primitives — semantically meaningful parameters that translate high-level reasoning into low-level behaviors. — https://arxiv.org/html/2602.02839

candidates.title: N3D-VLM: Native 3D Grounding Enables Accurate Spatial Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
candidates.summary: Integrates 3D detection, grounding, and chain-of-thought reasoning — enhances accuracy and interpretability by working with structured 3D bounding box representations. — https://arxiv.org/html/2512.16561v1

candidates.title: Simplify Generalist Robot Policy Evaluation in Simulation with NVIDIA Isaac Lab-Arena
candidates.summary: Scalable, repeatable simulation-based evaluation for generalist policies across diverse tasks, embodiments, and environments. — https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/simplify-generalist-robot-policy-evaluation-in-simulation-with-nvidia-isaac-lab-arena/

candidates.title: AI Agent Systems: Architectures, Applications, and Evaluation
candidates.summary: Survey covering memory architectures, tool use, critic/verifier patterns for consistency checking, and long-term memory for extended interaction. — https://arxiv.org/html/2601.01743v1

candidates.title: Reinforcement learning in robotic systems: A review on sim-to-real transfer
candidates.summary: Presents a sim-to-real RL framework for model optimization and iterative policy transfer; covers domain randomization, system identification, and domain adaptation. — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0921889025004245

theme: agent-to-action pipelines and world-model tooling — from embedded/IoT “agent skills” to tactile-first manipulation and policy context.

candidates.title: Skilled AI Agents for Embedded and IoT Systems Development
candidates.summary: Surveys/argues for “skilled” LLM agents in embedded/HIL/IoT development workflows—useful framing for robot-middleware style agent stacks. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.19583

candidates.title: World4RL: Diffusion World Models for Policy Refinement with Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.summary: Uses diffusion world models to refine manipulation policies with RL—interesting for imagination-driven policy improvement loops. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.19080

candidates.title: No Need to Look! Locating and Grasping Objects by a Robot Arm Covered with Sensitive Skin
candidates.summary: Tactile-first manipulation (skin-covered arm) that reduces reliance on vision—adjacent but potentially strong for robust household interaction. — https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.17986

candidates.title: China’s New Five-Year Plan Prioritizes Robotics. The World Should Pay Attention.
candidates.summary: Policy/standardization context for robotics scaling; weakly technical but can inform ecosystem/timing assumptions. — https://thediplomat.com/2026/03/chinas-new-five-year-plan-prioritizes-robotics-the-world-should-pay-attention/

candidates.title: DreamDojo Platform for Scalable Robot Training
candidates.summary: Secondary write-up about a “generalist robot world model” platform; may contain pointers but needs source-tracing. — https://www.labellerr.com/blog/dreamdojo-generalist-robot-world-model/

theme: turning language into physically grounded action via spatial memory, probabilistic grounding, and simulation-ready digital twins.

candidates.title: Meanings and Measurements: Multi-Agent Probabilistic Grounding for Vision-Language Navigation
candidates.summary: Agentic decomposition of metric+semantic language into grounded sub-queries, then probabilistic composition for metrically consistent actions; introduces MAPG-Bench + robot demo

candidates.title: GSMem: 3D Gaussian Splatting as Persistent Spatial Memory for Zero-Shot Embodied Exploration and Reasoning
candidates.summary: 3D Gaussian splatting as “re-observable” spatial memory; can render optimal novel views post-hoc to fix missed observations and improve VLM reasoning

candidates.title: MotionAnymesh: Physics-Grounded Articulation for Simulation-Ready Digital Twins
candidates.summary: Zero-shot mesh→articulated asset pipeline that grounds VLM part reasoning with physical priors and enforces collision-free articulation

candidates.title: CORE: Contextual Safety Reasoning and Grounding for Open-World Robots
candidates.summary: VLM infers context-dependent safety rules from vision, grounds spatial safe sets, enforces online with control barrier functions + uncertainty-aware guarantees

candidates.title: Asset-Centric Metric-Semantic Maps of Indoor Environments
candidates.summary: Explicit indoor map with object meshes+poses+categories (Unitree Go2 + RGB-D) positioned as a better mid-layer for LLM planning than scene-only semantics or object-only mapping

candidates.title: A Hyperbolic Perspective on Hierarchical Structure in Object-Centric Scene Representations
candidates.summary: Post-hoc projection of slot embeddings into hyperbolic space reveals latent scene→object hierarchies; suggests geometry as an inductive bias for multi-level world models

candidates.title: Newton 1.0 GA: GPU-accelerated physics engine for contact-rich manipulation & locomotion
candidates.summary: NVIDIA Developer Blog — Modular sim stack (URDF/MJCF/USD) unifying solvers + deformables; useful substrate for training policies and validating simulation-ready twins

candidates.title: World Models: Computing the Uncomputable
candidates.summary: Not Boring (Substack) — Broad but concrete synthesis of action-conditioned world models for planning; good landscape map of approaches and tradeoffs

theme: intent→plan→action loops are getting more *modular* and more *real-world benchmarkable* (digital twins, TAMP+FMs, and reproducible agent stacks).

candidates.title: TwinRL: Digital Twin-Driven Reinforcement Learning for Real-World Robotic Manipulation
candidates.summary: Reconstructs a high-fidelity “digital twin” from smartphone-captured scenes, then uses sim to expand exploration + guide online RL for VLA manipulation with minimal real-world time.

candidates.title: TiPToP: A Modular Open-Vocabulary Planning System for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.summary: Zero-robot-data modular stack (vision foundation models + task-and-motion planning) that matches/outscores a VLA fine-tuned on 350h DROID; open-source & component-level failure analysis.

candidates.title: AgenticLab: A Real-World Robot Agent Platform that Can See, Think, and Act
candidates.summary: Model-agnostic real-robot benchmark + closed-loop pipeline (perception → decomposition → verification → replanning) to expose long-horizon grounding failures that VQA-style tests miss.

candidates.title: Loc3R-VLM: Language-based Localization and 3D Reasoning with Vision-Language Models
candidates.summary: Adds explicit 3D spatial supervision from monocular video (global layout reconstruction + egocentric situation modeling) to make 2D VLMs viewpoint-aware for localization/3D QA.

candidates.title: Motion-MLLM: Egomotion-Aware Video Representation for Efficient and Accurate 3D Scene Understanding
candidates.summary: Injects IMU/egomotion tokens to ground absolute scale and spatial relations; proposes motion-visual keyframe filtering for better cost-effectiveness than heavy 3D reps.

candidates.title: MAS-Orchestra + MASBENCH: Understanding and Improving Multi-Agent Reasoning Through Holistic Orchestration and Controlled Benchmarks
candidates.summary: Treats MAS orchestration as function-calling RL and proposes a controlled benchmark (depth/horizon/breadth/parallel/robustness) to predict when multi-agent beats single-agent.

candidates.title: Latent Policy Steering (LPS) with Embodiment-Agnostic Pretrained World Models
candidates.summary: Pretrains a world model with optical-flow action reps across robots+humans, then uses the WM + value function to steer a base policy—helpful for low-data real-world regimes.

candidates.title: WorldVLM: Combining World Model Forecasting and Vision-Language Reasoning
candidates.summary: Hybrid VLM+world-model architecture: VLM emits high-level behavior commands, WM predicts dynamics. Presented in driving, but the pattern is a clean separation for embodied planners.

candidates.title: Causality Elicitation from Large Language Models
candidates.summary: Turns an LLM into an inspectable causal-hypothesis generator: sample docs → canonicalize events → run causal discovery to surface candidate causal graphs (not “true causality”, but useful).

candidates.title: Large Scale Robotic Material Handling: Learning, Planning, and Control
candidates.summary: Full-stack autonomy on a 40-ton hydraulic material handler (pile attack point selection + trajectory control for an underactuated gripper). A rare “industrial scale” embodied stack.

theme: intent → skills → control loops(how we turn language into robust robot behavior, with intermediate representations like hierarchical skills, scene graphs, and 3D grounding).

candidates.title: Hierarchical Open-Vocabulary 3D Scene Graphs for Language-Grounded Robot Navigation (HOV-SG)
candidates.summary: Compresses open-vocabulary maps into hierarchical scene graphs (object/room/floor) and demonstrates long-horizon language-conditioned navigation.

candidates.title: Inducing Causal World Models in LLMs for Zero-Shot Physical Reasoning
candidates.summary: Attempts to *inject causal structure* into LLM-style reasoning for physical scenarios; adjacent to “interventionist” world models for embodied agents.

candidates.title: Three-dimensional-grounded vision-language framework for robotic task planning: Automated prompt synthesis and supervised reasoning
candidates.summary: Practical path: frozen VLM + automated prompt synthesis + supervision to achieve high-precision 3D grounding for task planning.

candidates.title: Transformation of industrial robotics with natural language models: Recent progress and future prospects
candidates.summary: Industry-facing survey; useful to map research architectures to deployment constraints (safety, latency, integration).

theme: Intent-to-action pipeline tightens — hierarchical planners, spatial grounding, and world models converge.

candidates.title: Spatially Grounded Long-Horizon Task Planning in the Wild (GroundedPlanBench)
candidates.summary: Benchmark jointly evaluating action planning and spatial grounding; tests whether VLM-generated plans correspond to physically feasible robot manipulation. Built on DROID with 308 real-world scenes, 1K evaluation episodes.

candidates.title: GRHP: Graph-Fused Hierarchical Planning for Embodied Long-Horizon Robotic Tasks
candidates.summary: Dual-graph architecture — scene graph for spatial layouts + task graph for dependencies. Addresses dynamic replanning with explicit spatial associations between objects.

candidates.title: 3D-Grounded Vision-Language Framework for Robotic Task Planning
candidates.summary: Modular framework with automated prompt synthesis and supervised reasoning for 3D grounding. Ablation shows removing language model supervisor drops success rate by 67%.

candidates.title: LAC-WM: Latent Action Robot Foundation World Models for Cross-Embodiment Adaptation
candidates.summary: Robot foundation world model operating in learned unified latent action space shared across diverse embodiments. Explores how unified action space improves world model performance when adapted to unseen robot embodiments.

candidates.title: 4D Latent World Model for Robot Planning
candidates.summary: Serves as a planner, generating future scenes translated into executable actions via goal-conditioned inverse dynamics model. Demonstrates superior visual quality, physical consistency, and multi-view coherence.

candidates.title: V-JEPA 2: World Model and Physical Reasoning Benchmarks
candidates.summary: New V-JEPA 2 world model + benchmarks for physical reasoning. Robot plans by imagining consequences of candidate actions and rating them via model-predictive control toward desired goals.

candidates.title: Digital Twin based Automatic Reconfiguration of Robotic Systems
candidates.summary: Combines SLAM (spatial mapping) with path planning for automatic reconfiguration in smart environments.

candidates.title: A-RAG: Scaling Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation
candidates.summary: Hierarchical retrieval interfaces for agentic RAG. Two paradigms: multi-passage single-shot retrieval and step-by-step workflow execution.

candidates.title: MoWM: Mixture-of-World-Models for Embodied Planning
candidates.summary: Unsupervised world models trained on large-scale video learn universal dynamics. Maps predicted future state sequences to action sequences via inverse dynamics model.

candidates.title: AffordGrasp: In-Context Affordance Reasoning for Open-Vocabulary Task-Oriented Grasping
candidates.summary:
Framework identifies task-relevant objects, grounds part-level affordances via visual grounding module, generates task-oriented grasp poses within affordance regions.

---

theme: Intent-to-action pipelines take center stage — new work on hierarchical cognition, spatial grounding benchmarks, and sim-to-real transfer advances.

candidates.title: RoboCasa365: A Large-Scale Simulation Framework for Training and Benchmarking Generalist Robots
candidates.summary: 365 days of simulated household scenarios for training generalist robots at scale. Tests progress toward everyday task execution.

candidates.title: CAIMAN: Causal Action Influence Detection for Sample-Efficient Loco-Manipulation
candidates.summary: Hierarchical framework for whole-body object pushing with legged robots using causal influence as intrinsic motivation.

candidates.title: Ctrl-World: A Controllable Generative World Model for Robot Manipulation
candidates.summary: Multi-view world model with pose-conditioned memory retrieval and frame-level action conditioning for precise instruction-following.

candidates.title: H-WM: Robotic Task and Motion Planning Guided by Hierarchical World Model
candidates.summary: Addresses LLM planner limitations (language as interface → semantic-execution misalignment) with world-model-based predictive visual guidance.

candidates.title: Beyond Dense Futures: World Models as Structured Planners for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.summary: Two-stage training with discrete token vocabulary — world model predicts structured frames, then maps foresight to low-level actions.

candidates.title: RoboMME: Benchmarking and Understanding Memory for Robotic Generalist Policies
candidates.summary: Four memory dimensions (temporal, spatial, object, procedural) framework for evaluating history-dependent manipulation policies.

candidates.title: GeCo-SRT: Geometry-aware Continual Adaptation for Robotic Cross-Task Sim-to-Real Transfer
candidates.summary: Geometry-aware adaptation method for transferring policies across tasks from simulation to real robots.

theme: World models graduate from dense prediction to structured planning, while multi-agent hierarchies learn to optimize their own prompts.

candidates.title: StructVLA: Beyond Dense Futures — World Models as Structured Planners for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.summary: Replaces dense future-frame rollouts with sparse, kinematically meaningful "structured frames" (gripper transitions, turning points) that bridge visual planning and low-level action. 75% on SimplerEnv-WidowX, 94.8% on LIBERO.

candidates.title: Hierarchical LLM-Based Multi-Agent Framework with Prompt Optimization for Multi-Robot Task Planning
candidates.summary: Upper-layer LLM decomposes tasks and assigns to lower-layer agents that generate PDDL problems. TextGrad-inspired textual-gradient updates optimize each agent's prompt. SOTA on MAT-THOR: 0.95/0.84/0.60 on compound/complex/vague tasks.

candidates.title: Cognition to Control (C2C): Multi-Agent Learning for Human-Humanoid Collaborative Transport
candidates.summary: Three-layer hierarchy: VLM grounding → deliberative skill/coordination layer (MARL as Markov potential game) → whole-body control. Translates human intent into contact-stable humanoid motion for collaborative transport tasks.

candidates.title: Multimodal Behavior Tree Generation: A Small VLM for Robot Task Planning
candidates.summary: Fine-tunes compact VLMs (500M–4B params) to generate executable BehaviorTree.CPP behavior trees from RGB observations + instructions. 4B model hits 87% success rate on household tasks, matching closed-source models at a fraction of compute.

candidates.title: IMR-LLM: Industrial Multi-Robot Task Planning and Program Generation
candidates.summary: LLM constructs disjunctive graphs for industrial multi-robot scheduling, then uses process trees to guide executable low-level program generation. Includes IMR-Bench for industrial multi-robot task evaluation.

candidates.title: Reflective Test-Time Planning for Embodied LLMs
candidates.summary: Introduces reflection-in-action (test-time scaling to score candidate actions before execution) and reflection-on-action (test-time training to update policy after execution). Includes retrospective re-evaluation for long-horizon credit assignment. Real-robot trials.

candidates.title: InSpatio-WorldFM: Open-Source Real-Time 3D World Model
candidates.summary: Open-source generative frame model for real-time 3D scene generation on consumer GPUs. Multi-view consistent, spatially-aware — designed for robotics, simulation, and XR edge deployment. Not a paper, but a released model.

candidates.title: Latent Particle World Models (LPWM): Self-supervised Object-centric Stochastic Dynamics Modeling
candidates.summary: Self-supervised discovery of keypoints, bounding boxes, and object masks directly from video. End-to-end trained object-centric world model supporting unconditional video prediction and goal-conditioned imitation learning.

candidates.title: GeoAgentic-RAG: Multi-Agent Framework for Autonomous Geospatial Reasoning
candidates.summary: Multi-agent framework combining multimodal LLMs with spatial-semantic retrieval for geospatial question answering. Addresses the core limitation that text-based similarity can't represent topology and spatial context. Adjacent to indoor spatial RAG.

candidates.title: Ego-Vision World Model for Humanoid Contact Planning
candidates.summary: Learned world model + sampling-based MPC for humanoid contact planning. Trained on demonstration-free offline data; handles wall support, object blocking, arch traversal. Deployed on physical humanoid with ego-centric depth.

theme: Agentic frameworks are converging on robot manipulation — this week's standout papers show LLMs not just planning, but rewriting their own low-level controllers, self-resetting for data collection, and closing the loop from intent to physical action.

candidates.title: RoboClaw: An Agentic Framework for Scalable Long-Horizon Robotic Tasks
candidates.summary: Unifies data collection, policy learning, and deployment under a single VLM-driven controller using Entangled Action Pairs for autonomous self-resetting loops — 25% success rate improvement on long-horizon tasks with 53.7% less human effort.

candidates.title: AOR: Multimodal Coding Agents as In-Context Policy Learners for Robot Manipulation
candidates.summary: LLM agent improves manipulation policy by rewriting its own Python controller code between trials — no demonstrations, no reward engineering, no gradient updates. The full motor control implementation is the unit of reasoning.

candidates.title: HALO: A Unified Vision-Language-Action Model for Embodied Multimodal Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
candidates.summary: Unified VLA enabling sequential textual task reasoning → visual subgoal prediction → action prediction via Mixture-of-Transformers architecture. Includes automated EM-CoT data synthesis pipeline. Strong on long-horizon and OOD scenarios.

candidates.title: RoboCritics: Enabling Reliable End-to-End LLM Robot Programming through Expert-Informed Critics
candidates.summary: Augments LLM-based robot programming with expert critics that analyze motion traces for safety violations (joint speeds, collisions, unsafe poses) and offer one-click fixes. User study (n=18) shows reduced safety violations and better execution quality vs. baseline LLM interface.

candidates.title: HiTOC: Hierarchical Task-Oriented Communication for Long-Horizon Tasks
candidates.summary: Hierarchical planner-actor architecture where robot transmits only subtask-relevant observations to edge server. Conditional variational information bottleneck minimizes bandwidth per subtask. Outperforms SOTA on MAP-THOR benchmark.

candidates.title: Trajectory-Informed Memory Generation for Self-Improving Agent Systems
candidates.summary: Framework for extracting structured learnings from agent execution trajectories — strategy tips from successes, recovery tips from failures, optimization tips from inefficiencies. Up to 14.3pp gains on AppWorld benchmark; 149% relative improvement on complex tasks.

candidates.title: MANGO: Sim2real Image Translation Enables Viewpoint-Robust Policies from Fixed-Camera Datasets
candidates.summary: Unpaired image translation with segmentation-conditioned InfoNCE loss bridges sim-to-real visual gap while maintaining viewpoint consistency. Increases shifted-view success rates by 40+ percentage points using only small fixed-camera real-world datasets.

candidates.title: Test-Driven Agentic Framework for Reliable Robot Controller Synthesis
candidates.summary: Test-driven LLM agent iteratively refines generated controller code via structured test suites. Dual-tier repair strategy (prompt-level + direct code editing). Substantially improves reliability over one-shot generation especially for underspecified prompts.

candidates.title: When World Models Dream Wrong: Physical-Conditioned Adversarial Attacks against World Models
candidates.summary: First white-box attack on generative world models via perturbed physical-condition channels (HDMap, 3D-box features). Downstream impact: 4% drop in 3D detection, 20% worse planning. Important for world-model reliability in any embodied system.

candidates.title: Federated Causal Representation Learning for Decentralized Counterfactual Reasoning
candidates.summary: Each client learns latent states disentangling intrinsic dynamics from control influences; central server estimates global transition structure. Enables cross-client counterfactual queries ("what if client B operated differently?") without sharing raw data. Interesting paradigm for distributed robot fleets.

theme: The intent-to-action pipeline matures — from VLM grounding layers and hierarchical skill coordination to world models that actually work as simulators, plus a pragmatic VLA that scales to 20K hours of real-world data.

candidates.title: Cognition to Control (C2C): Multi-Agent Learning for Human-Humanoid Collaborative Transport
candidates.summary: Three-layer hierarchy (VLM grounding → deliberative MARL skill layer → whole-body control) bridging System 2 deliberation with low-latency execution; emergent leader-follower behaviors

candidates.title: LingBot-VLA: A Pragmatic VLA Foundation Model
candidates.summary: 20K hours real-world dual-arm data across 9 robot configs; SOTA on 100 tasks per platform with 130 post-training episodes; open-source codebase (261 samples/sec, 1.5–2.8× speedup)

candidates.title: AGPS: Agent-Guided Policy Search — Accelerating Robotic RL with Agent Guidance
candidates.summary: Accelerating Robotic RL with Agent Guidance** — arXiv (cs.RO, cs.AI) — Replaces human supervisors in HIL RL with multimodal agent as semantic world model; corrective waypoints and exploration pruning; outperforms HIL on deformable objects

candidates.title: Automating Skill Acquisition from Open-Source Agentic Repositories
candidates.summary: Framework mining GitHub repos to extract procedural skills into SKILL.md format; 40% gains in knowledge transfer efficiency; the SKILL.md spec as universal agent capability format

candidates.title: NovaPlan: Zero-Shot Long-Horizon Manipulation via Closed-Loop Video Language Planning
candidates.summary: Hierarchical VLM + video planning with geometrically grounded execution; autonomous re-planning on failure using object keypoints and hand poses from generated videos

theme: World models are reaching deployment maturity — from edge-native embodied brains to $1B JEPA bets — while VLA architectures splinter into guidance-enhanced and minimal-baseline camps.

candidates.title: DreamZero: World Action Models are Zero-shot Policies
candidates.summary: 14B-parameter World Action Model that jointly models video and action via diffusion, enabling zero-shot robot policies; cross-embodiment transfer with only 30 min of play data

candidates.title: OmniGuide: Universal Guidance Fields for Enhancing Generalist Robot Policies
candidates.summary: Inference-time guidance framework that steers VLA policies (π₀.₅, GR00T N1.6) using differentiable 3D energy fields from foundation models and VLMs

candidates.title: ACE Robotics Open-Sources Kairos 3.0-4B
candidates.summary: ACE Robotics / Zawya — First native embodied world model with unified multi-modal understanding-generation-prediction; 4B params, 72x faster than Cosmos 2.5, real-time edge on Jetson Thor

candidates.title: VLM-PoseManip: Dexterous Manipulation via VLM-based Instructive Pose Estimation for HRC
candidates.summary: Unified framework bridging high-level instruction understanding and physically grounded dexterous execution for human-robot collaboration in unstructured settings

theme: World models learning from autonomous play, control theory meets agentic AI, and memory architectures for personalized embodied agents.

candidates.title: PlayWorld: Learning Robot World Models from Autonomous Play
candidates.summary: First system to learn video world models entirely from unsupervised robot self-play rather than human demos. RL in the world model improves real-world policy success by 65%. Directly addresses sim-to-real gap via scalable autonomous data collection.

candidates.title: A Control-Theoretic Foundation for Agentic Systems
candidates.summary: Formalizes agentic AI as hierarchical control loops with 5 levels of agency — from reactive rules to self-synthesizing control objectives. Provides mathematical stability/safety analysis framework for AI-in-the-loop robot control. A rare rigorous foundation for what's usually hand-wavy.

candidates.title: DM0: An Embodied-Native Vision-Language-Action Model towards Physical AI
candidates.summary: Purpose-built VLA model (not adapted from chat LLM) using Qwen3-1.7B backbone with dedicated perception encoder. Designed ground-up for embodied reasoning + manipulation action output. Represents the shift from "VLM + action wrapper" to "embodied-native" architectures.

candidates.title: MEMENTO: Embodied Agents Meet Personalization via Memory Utilization
candidates.summary: Benchmark + hierarchical knowledge-graph memory for personalized embodied agents. Agents can recall object semantics but fail at applying sequential user patterns. Proposes episodic memory → KG user-profile architecture. Directly relevant to spatial RAG for personalized robot task planning.

candidates.title: IntentCUA: Intent-Level Skill Abstraction for Multi-Agent Planning
candidates.summary: Multi-agent framework where Planner, Plan-Optimizer, and Critic share intent-abstracted memory. Reduces redundant re-planning via skill retrieval from intent prototypes. 74.8% task success on long-horizon desktop tasks. Transferable architecture for robot task decomposition.

candidates.title: HMR-1: Hierarchical Massage Robot with VLM for Embodied Healthcare
candidates.summary: VLM-based acupoint grounding + low-level trajectory control for massage robots. Includes MedMassage-12K dataset (12K images, 174K QA pairs). Unusual but demonstrates the high-level grounding → low-level control pattern in a novel domain.

candidates.title: Speech-Omni-Lite: Portable Speech Interfaces for VLMs
candidates.summary: Plug-and-play speech modules that bolt onto any frozen VL backbone — adds speech understanding + generation without retraining. Achieves parity with models trained on millions of hours using only thousands. Relevant for voice-commanded robot interfaces.

candidates.title: CARAG: Context-Aware RAG over Spatial Knowledge Graphs
candidates.summary: RAG framework treating spatial data + documents as unified knowledge substrate via spatial knowledge graph with concept and instance levels. Applied to railway O&M but the architecture — spatial KG as RAG source for context-aware retrieval — maps directly to indoor robot spatial reasoning.

candidates.title: Simon Willison's Agentic Engineering Patterns
candidates.summary: Practitioner's pattern catalog for coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) — red/green TDD, linear codebase walkthroughs, cost-of-code recalibration. Not robotics, but the engineering patterns (test-first agent loops, supervisor patterns) transfer to agentic robot middleware design.

candidates.title: A Natural Behavior Planner for Multi-Personal HRI in Simulation
candidates.summary:
Extends affordance reasoning beyond physical manipulation to social cues and contextual roles in multi-person HRI. Robot must perceive not just what actions objects afford, but how it ought to behave in social context. Bridges affordance theory with social robot behavior planning.

---

*Generated by research-agent cron, 2026-03-13 08:00 CET*

theme: Object-centric world models get real; spatial grounding closes the loop from perception to manipulation; the VC consensus on world models crystallizes around the data-vs-physics inflection.

candidates.title: Latent Particle World Models (LPWM): Self-supervised Object-centric Stochastic Dynamics Modeling
candidates.summary: Self-supervised object-centric world model that discovers keypoints, bboxes, and masks from video; models stochastic particle dynamics and supports goal-conditioned imitation learning.

candidates.title: RAGNav: Retrieval-Augmented Topological Reasoning for Multi-Goal Visual-Language Navigation
candidates.summary: RAG framework for multi-goal indoor navigation with dual-basis memory (topological map + semantic forest), enabling spatial-aware retrieval and sequential planning in complex indoor environments.

candidates.title: DACo: Global Commander and Local Operative — Dual-Agent Framework for Scene Navigation
candidates.summary: Decouples global deliberation from local grounding via a dual-agent architecture. Global Commander plans high-level strategy; Local Operative handles egocentric perception and execution. 4.9-6.5% improvements over baselines in zero-shot VLN.

candidates.title: ABot-M0: VLA Foundation Model for Robotic Manipulation with Action Manifold Learning
candidates.summary: "One-brain, many-forms" VLA that learns a unified action manifold from 6M+ trajectories across diverse robot morphologies. Proposes Action Manifold Hypothesis: effective robot actions lie on a low-dimensional manifold, not in full action space.

candidates.title: Can World Models Unlock General Purpose Robotics?
candidates.summary: VC deep-dive comparing the world model moment to NLP's 2005 inflection. Key stat: V-JEPA 2 takes ~16s/action; real-time needs 100x speedup. Frames the data gap as fundamentally harder than LLMs' internet bootstrapping.

candidates.title: MV-RoboBench: Seeing Across Views — Benchmarking Spatial Reasoning of VLMs in Robotic Scenes
candidates.summary: Multi-view spatial reasoning benchmark for VLMs and VLAs. Key finding: strong single-view VLM scores don't transfer to multi-view robotic tasks — spatial intelligence and task execution are correlated only in multi-view settings.

candidates.title: GeCo-SRT: Geometry-aware Continual Adaptation for Robotic Cross-Task Sim-to-Real Transfer
candidates.summary: Continual sim-to-real transfer that accumulates geometric knowledge across tasks via mixture-of-experts. 52% avg improvement over baseline, only 1/6 data needed for new task adaptation. Reframes sim-to-real as knowledge accumulation, not isolated transfer.

candidates.title: Where Bits Matter in World Model Planning: Mixed-Bit Quantization for Spatial Reasoning
candidates.summary: Studies how bit allocation across world model modules affects spatial planning. Finds a critical 4-bit transition regime where encoder precision matters most — practical insight for deploying world models on edge robotics hardware.

candidates.title: Latent World Models for Automated Driving: A Unified Taxonomy
candidates.summary: Comprehensive taxonomy of latent-space world models for driving — but the framework (latent worlds/actions/generators, structural priors, five cross-cutting mechanics) transfers directly to indoor robotics world models. Good architectural reference.

candidates.title: Where Autonomy Works: Evaluating Robot Capabilities in 2026
candidates.summary: Rigorous assessment: navigation is deployed, manipulation works only in controlled settings, transfer is rarely demonstrated. Foundation models are now default for manipulation but speed (3-10x slower than humans) isn't the main bottleneck — generalization is.

theme: World models are becoming practical robot simulators, while spatial reasoning benchmarks expose how far VLMs still have to go — today's batch spans interactive world simulation for policy training, multi-agent manipulation orchestration, VLM-driven humanoid control, and several new benchmarks probing spatial intelligence in indoor scenes.

candidates.title: Interactive World Simulator for Robot Policy Training and Evaluation
candidates.summary: Consistency-model-based world simulator that runs at 15 FPS on a single RTX 4090, enabling 10+ minute stable rollouts; policies trained purely on world-model-generated data match real-world-trained policies.

candidates.title: MetaWorld-X: Hierarchical World Modeling via VLM-Orchestrated Experts for Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
candidates.summary: Decomposes humanoid whole-body control into specialized expert policies trained under human motion priors, then uses a VLM to dynamically compose them for multi-stage loco-manipulation tasks.

candidates.title: MALLVI: A Multi-Agent Framework for Integrated Generalized Robotics Manipulation
candidates.summary: Multi-agent VLM framework (Decomposer, Localizer, Thinker, Reflector) for closed-loop robotic manipulation; the Reflector enables targeted error recovery by reactivating only relevant agents instead of full replanning.

candidates.title: FindAnything: Open-Vocabulary and Object-Centric Mapping for Robot Exploration in Any Environment
candidates.summary: Real-time open-vocabulary object-centric volumetric mapping using eSAM + VL features; faster and more memory-efficient than SOTA, deployable on resource-constrained robots including MAVs.

candidates.title: SpatiaLQA: A Benchmark for Evaluating Spatial Logical Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
candidates.summary: 9,605 QA pairs from 241 real indoor scenes testing spatial *logical* reasoning (multi-step spatial dependencies); proposes recursive scene graph reasoning that outperforms all 41 tested VLMs.

candidates.title: SpatiaLab: Can Vision-Language Models Perform Spatial Reasoning in the Wild?
candidates.summary: 1,400 VQA pairs across 30 task types (depth/occlusion, orientation, 3D geometry, navigation) in realistic unconstrained scenes; exposes major VLM weaknesses in wild spatial reasoning.

candidates.title: Object-Aware Semantic Mapping Using Probability Density Functions for Indoor Relocalization and Path Planning
candidates.summary: Probabilistic object-centric semantic map representation for indoor robots; unifies relocalization and path planning in a single object-level representation validated in a real apartment.

candidates.title: AVIC: Adaptive Test-Time Scaling with World Models for Visual Spatial Reasoning
candidates.summary: Studies *when* and *how much* visual imagination via world models actually helps spatial reasoning; introduces adaptive framework that matches fixed-imagination strategies with far fewer world-model calls.

candidates.title: ReAcTree: Hierarchical LLM Agent Trees with Control Flow for Long-Horizon Task Planning
candidates.summary: Hierarchical agent tree that decomposes long-horizon goals into subgoals handled by specialized LLM nodes with episodic + working memory; nearly doubles ReAct's success rate on WAH-NL (31% → 61%).

candidates.title: Towards Learning a Generalizable 3D Scene Representation from 2D Observations
candidates.summary: Generalizable NeRF for predicting 3D workspace occupancy in a global frame from egocentric robot views; 26mm reconstruction error including occluded regions, trained on only 40 real scenes.

theme: Agentic robot middleware goes real-world: robot-agnostic control, spatial memory retrieval, and the honest fragility of LLM-driven physical systems.

candidates.title: RACAS: Controlling Diverse Robots With a Single Agentic System
candidates.summary: Three LLM/VLM modules (Monitors, Controller, Memory Curator) communicate via natural language for closed-loop robot control — zero retraining across wheeled, multi-jointed limb, and underwater platforms. Only needs NL description of robot + action defs.

candidates.title: MoMa-SG: Articulated 3D Scene Graphs for Open-World Mobile Manipulation
candidates.summary: Builds semantic-kinematic 3D scene graphs of articulated indoor environments — bridges semantics, geometry, and kinematics for long-horizon mobile manipulation. Includes Arti4D-Semantic dataset (62 RGB-D sequences, 600 object interactions). Validated on quadruped + mobile manipulator.

candidates.title: STaR: Scalable Task-Conditioned Retrieval for Long-Horizon Multimodal Robot Memory
candidates.summary: Agentic reasoning framework for robot memory: builds task-agnostic multimodal long-term memory, then uses Information Bottleneck-based retrieval for compact, non-redundant memory snippets. MLLM agent plans search strategy + issues API calls to retrieve spatial/temporal/semantic memory. Deployed on real Husky robot.

candidates.title: AgentRob: From Virtual Forum Agents to Hijacked Physical Robots
candidates.summary: Bridges online forums, LLM agents, and physical Unitree Go2/G1 robots via MCP (Model Context Protocol). Forum agents poll for mentions, extract NL commands, dispatch robot actions, report back. Three-layer architecture (Forum → Agent → Robot). VLM-driven controllers expose robot primitives as callable tools.

candidates.title: Agentic AI for Robot Control: Flexible but still Fragile
candidates.summary: Honest stress-test of agentic robot control on two physical platforms (indoor mobile manipulation Mobipick + agricultural robot Valdemar). Reveals substantial fragility: non-deterministic behavior, instruction-following errors, prompt sensitivity. Supports operator intervention and structured introspection.

candidates.title: PragmaBot: A Pragmatist Robot — Learning to Plan Tasks by Experiencing the Real World
candidates.summary: Verbal RL without parameter updates — VLM acts as robot brain+eye, self-reflects on failures stored in short-term memory, distills lessons to long-term memory, uses RAG for new tasks. STM-based reflection: 35%→84% success. LTM+RAG: 22%→80% single-trial success across 12 real-world scenarios.

candidates.title: Unveiler: Learning Object-Centric Spatial Reasoning for Sequential Manipulation in Cluttered Environments
candidates.summary: Decouples high-level spatial reasoning from low-level action execution via lightweight transformer-based Spatial Relationship Encoder. 97.6% success (partial occlusion), 90% (full occlusion) in simulation. SRE transfers zero-shot to real scenes — no retraining for physical robot.

candidates.title: Continual Learning and Refinement of Causal Models through Dynamic Predicate Invention
candidates.summary: Builds symbolic causal world models entirely online using Meta-Interpretive Learning + predicate invention. Creates reusable abstractions from agent observations within the decision loop. Lifted inference scales to complex relational dynamics; sample-efficiency orders of magnitude better than PPO baselines.

candidates.title: What Matters in Building Vision-Language-Action Models for Generalist Robots
candidates.summary: Nature MI paper systematically studying VLA design choices: backbone selection, architecture formulation, cross-embodiment data. Comes with RoboVLMs codebase (GitHub). Authoritative reference for the VLA design space.

candidates.title: OpenClaw Gives Unitree Robots "World Memory" & Spatial Understanding
candidates.summary:
OpenClaw's Spatial Agent Memory + SpatialRAG constructs a voxelized world from LiDAR, stereo cameras, RGB, and odometry. Each voxel tagged with spatial vector embeddings + semantic labels. Hardware-agnostic, ROS-free. Demonstrated on Unitree humanoids for spatiotemporal recall of people, objects, events.

---

theme: The VLA-World-Model marriage matures — three papers independently discover that jointly training world models and VLA policies in closed loops produces dramatic real-world gains, while spatial grounding becomes the secret ingredient connecting vision-language understanding to precise manipulation.

candidates.title: WoVR: World Models as Reliable Simulators for Post-Training VLA Policies with RL
candidates.summary: World-model RL framework for VLA post-training; keyframe-initialized rollouts + co-evolution tame hallucination; LIBERO 40→69%, real-robot 62→92%

candidates.title: World-VLA-Loop: Closed-Loop Learning of Video World Model and VLA Policy
candidates.summary: Co-evolves video world model and VLA in closed loop; failure rollouts refine world model which improves RL; 36.7% real-world improvement after two iterations

candidates.title: ST4VLA: Spatially Guided Training for Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.summary: Dual-system VLA separating where/what from how via spatial grounding pre-training then spatially guided action post-training; new SOTA on SimplerEnv (66→85 Google Robot)

candidates.title: MICoBot: Mixed-Initiative Dialog for Human-Robot Collaborative Manipulation
candidates.summary: Three-level mixed-initiative system where both human and robot take initiative via NL; allocates steps by affordance model + human availability; real trials with 18 participants

candidates.title: Physics-Consistent Real-to-Sim for Highly Cluttered Environments
candidates.summary: Reconstructs physically consistent 3D scenes from single RGB-D via differentiable optimization with contact graph; solves floating-object problems for downstream manipulation sim

theme: Causal world models and object-level interventionism take center stage, with major new work on latent-space causal inductive bias, hierarchical planning, and predictive inverse dynamics.

theme: Causal world models meet inverse dynamics — from video prediction to actionable robot control

candidates.title: Causal World Modeling for Robot Control
candidates.summary: LingBot-VA: Vision-language action diffusion model with autoregressive causal world models. Integrates video world modeling as an independent foundation for robot learning (alongside vision-language pretraining). Enables long-horizon rollouts with closed-loop error correction during execution.

candidates.title: Predictive Inverse Dynamics Models are Scalable Learners
candidates.summary: PIDM (Seer): Predicts actions using inverse dynamics models conditioned on forecasted visual states. End-to-end paradigm that bridges vision pretraining and action prediction — unifies "vision" and "action" scaling paths in manipulation.

candidates.title: A-MEM: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents
candidates.summary: Autonomous contextual memory generation + intelligent connection with existing memories. Combines Zettelkasten structure with agent-driven decision making. Enables continuous memory evolution through new experiences and higher-order attributes.

candidates.title: 4D Latent World Model for Robot Planning
candidates.summary: 4D latent world model as planner — generates future scenes with multi-view coherence and physical consistency. Paired with goal-conditioned inverse dynamics model to translate planned futures into executable actions. Novel 4D approach (spatial + temporal).

candidates.title: AMA-Bench: Evaluating Long-Horizon Memory for Agentic Applications
candidates.summary: First benchmark for memory in agent applications. Two subsets: real-world machine-generated patterns + synthetic scalable horizon/complexity. Introduces Hybrid Tool-Augmented Retrieval to transcend similarity-based retrieval limitations. --- ## STRONG SIGNALS

candidates.title: 3D-Grounded Vision-Language Framework for Robotic Task Planning
candidates.summary: Automated prompt synthesis + supervised reasoning for frozen VLMs to achieve high-precision 3D grounding. 67% improvement in task planning accuracy. Demonstrates that frozen models can ground spatially via prompt engineering + expert supervision.

candidates.title: GEN-0: Embodied Foundation Models That Scale with Physical Interaction
candidates.summary: New class of embodied foundation models for multimodal training on high-fidelity raw physical interaction. Establishes scaling laws for robot intelligence (like LLMs). Architecture + data engine for behavioral generalization that grows with real-world experience.

candidates.title: SpatialLM: Training LLMs for Structured Indoor Modeling
candidates.summary: 3D LLM that processes point cloud data and generates structured 3D scene understanding outputs. Demonstrates feasibility of training LLMs for structured indoor modeling — a step toward foundation models that reason about, interact with, and create 3D scenes.

candidates.title: WoW: World-omniscient World-model Through Embodied Interaction
candidates.summary: SOPHIA: VLM agents evaluate DiT-generated output and guide refinement by iteratively evolving language instruction. Co-trained Inverse Dynamics Model translates plans into robotic actions. WoWBench benchmark for physical consistency and causal reasoning. Strong on physical causality, collision dynamics, object permanence.

candidates.title: The Essential Role of Causality in Foundation World Models for Embodied AI
candidates.summary: Theoretical analysis: gap between causal reasoning guarantees and practical needs in foundation models. Addresses tension between domain-specific causal identification and general-environment foundation models. Critical for safety-aware embodied AI. ---

theme: From vision to intent: new methods ground human language and spatial reasoning into robot action.

candidates.title: CASPER: Cognitive Architecture for Social Perception and Engagement in Robots
candidates.summary: Springer — Symbolic cognitive architecture for intention reading and collaborative behavior calculation in human-robot teaming scenarios

candidates.title: 4D Latent World Model for Robot Planning
candidates.summary: Uses 4D latent world model as planner, generates future scenes translated into executable actions via goal-conditioned inverse dynamics

candidates.title: A Study of the Framework and Real-World Applications of Language Embedding for 3D Scene Understanding
candidates.summary: Integrates LLMs and language embeddings into Gaussian Splatting for text-conditioned generation, editing, semantic scene understanding

candidates.title: Towards Efficient LLM Grounding for Embodied Multi-Agent Collaboration
candidates.summary: Novel feedback mechanism for closed-loop LLM planning in multi-agent collaboration, addresses grounding complexity via communication and credit assignment

candidates.title: Grounded Decoding: Guiding Text Generation
candidates.summary: Guides LLM output directly at generation for robot planning, enables grounding with embodiment constraints, safety functions, general planning algorithms

theme: Intent-to-action pathways, world models for planning, omni-modal orchestration — the human-robot ecosystem layer is emerging.

candidates.title: Planning with affordances: Integrating learned affordance models and symbolic planning
candidates.summary: Affordance models define what actions are possible in a state; integrated with symbolic planning for feasible task plans.

candidates.title: FindingDory: A Benchmark to Evaluate Memory in Embodied Agents
candidates.summary: 60 navigation tasks testing long-horizon memory in realistic indoor environments; measures efficiency beyond simple recall.

candidates.title: VLingNav: Embodied Navigation with Adaptive Reasoning and Visual-Assisted Linguistic Memory
candidates.summary: Visual-assisted linguistic memory enables long-horizon spatial reasoning; prevents repetitive exploration.

candidates.title: 4D Latent World Model for Robot Planning
candidates.summary: Generates future scenes with visual quality and multi-view coherence; translated into actions via goal-conditioned inverse dynamics.

candidates.title: LaMI: Large Language Models for Multi-Modal Human-Robot Interaction
candidates.summary: Regulates robot behavior via high-level NL guidance, atomic actions/expressions, and examples; systematizes LLM-based HRI.

theme: Bridging Intent and Action — Unified multimodal models, affordance-based planning, and memory-efficient spatial understanding systems drive toward seamless human-robot collaboration.

candidates.title: OmniVLA: An Omni-Modal Vision-Language-Action Model for Robot Navigation
candidates.summary: First VLA model unifying language, spatial coordinates, and visual goals for flexible robot navigation; enables robots to interpret multiple goal specifications like humans.

candidates.title: SpatialLM: Training Large Language Models for Structured Indoor Modeling
candidates.summary: 3D LLM processing point clouds to generate structured scene understanding; NeurIPS 2025, targets indoor layout reconstruction from spatial data.

candidates.title: SceneVGGT: VGGT-based online 3D semantic SLAM for indoor scene understanding
candidates.summary: Memory-efficient semantic SLAM using Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer with sliding window and temporal coherence; enabling real-time indoor navigation.

candidates.title: LaMI: Large Language Models for Multi-Modal Human-Robot Interaction
candidates.summary: Framework regulating robot behavior through high-level natural language guidance, atomic action definitions, and examples; published Jan 2026.

candidates.title: Planning with affordances: Integrating learned affordance models and symbolic planning
candidates.summary: Hybrid system combining learned affordance models (what actions possible + how to perform) with symbolic planning for goal achievement; Feb 2025.

candidates.title: CWM: Contrastive World Models for Action Feasibility Learning in Embodied Agent Pipelines
candidates.summary: Intrinsic affordance evaluation using contrastive world models to filter feasible actions in embodied agent task execution; published Feb 2026.

theme: Foundation models meet embodied intelligence—from robotic world models to agentic memory architectures.
